topic: My phone's location history shows me visiting an abandoned house every night at 3AM. I have never been there.
title: My Phone Shows Me Visiting an Abandoned House Every Night at 3AM | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My phone shows me visiting an abandoned house every night at 3AM. I have never been there.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a phone screen showing a location history map with a red pin on a dark street, repeated visits marked, dim room light, hand holding the phone, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I noticed it three weeks ago while checking my location history.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a phone screen with a list of location history timestamps all reading 3:00AM, the same address repeating down the screen, dark ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The coordinates point to a condemned house on Millfield Road. Empty since 2019.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior shot of a two-story abandoned house at night, windows boarded, overgrown yard, a single streetlight casting pale light on the front, no people, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I drove past it. Someone had cleared a fresh path through the weeds to the front door.
scenes.image_prompt: close ground-level shot of a narrow path worn through tall dead grass leading to a boarded wooden door, night exterior, moonlight from above, no people, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I searched the address. A family of four lived there. They disappeared in 2018.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing a local news article with the headline FAMILY VANISHES FROM HOME, the house visible in the article photo matching the abandoned structure, dark room, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The visits in my history go back fourteen months. Before I moved to this city.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a phone screen scrolling through months of location history, the same address highlighted repeatedly in a different city label, dim light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I called my carrier. They said the location data does not come from the phone. It comes from whatever pinged the tower.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a hand holding a phone to an ear, the face not visible, dark hallway background, a single lamp light from the next room, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Someone with my SIM number was at that house. Every night. For over a year.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior shot of the abandoned house at night from across the street, all windows dark and boarded, the worn path through the grass faintly visible, no movement, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I got a new number. New phone. New carrier.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a new phone box on a table beside an old phone, both illuminated by a single desk lamp, the old phone face down, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The visits stopped for eleven days.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a new phone screen showing a clean location history with no late night visits, a hand holding it, soft room light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last night my new phone showed the same address at 3AM.
scenes.image_prompt: extreme close up of a phone screen at 3:00AM showing the familiar address pinned on the map, screen glow in total darkness, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I checked my bedroom camera. I left at 2:47AM and returned at 3:14AM. I do not remember any of it.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing grainy security camera footage of an empty bed, the timestamp reading 2:47AM, the sheets disturbed, the room dark, no person visible in the frame, vertical 9:16 framing
